rows.label: statement
rows.left: He **should have called** the doctor earlier.

rows.label: negative
rows.left: She **shouldn't have left** without saying goodbye.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Remember that some verbs have irregular past participle forms (e.g. go → gone, take → taken, write → written).

content.subtitle: Use
items: To say someone had the chance or the ability to do something in the past but did not — **could have**: I **could have won** the race, but I tripped near the finish line.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Use **could + bare infinitive** for a general past ability: When I was ten, I **could swim** quite well.
Use **could have + past participle** for an opportunity that existed in the past but was not taken: I **could have swum** in the competition, but I chose not to.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To criticise a past action or say what the right thing to do would have been — **should have / ought to have**: You **ought to have checked** the instructions before you started.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Strong probability — **must have / can't have**: She was laughing so much — it **must have been** a really funny film. / He **can't have eaten** — the food is still untouched.
Possibility — **could have / may have / might have**: They left very early, so they **might have taken** the wrong train.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To express that something was expected to happen in the past but did not — **should have / ought to have**: The delivery **should have arrived** by yesterday — I ordered it a week ago.
